skill.summary: Generate AI-powered podcast-style audio narratives using Azure OpenAI's GPT Realtime Mini model via ...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 23 potential issues, but evaluation confirms all are false positives. The skill uses environment variables for Azure API authentication (legitimate), makes network calls to Azure OpenAI WebSocket endpoint (expected for TTS functionality), and uses base64 encoding for audio data transfer (standard practice). No malicious intent or data exfiltration patterns found. This is a legitimate podcast generation skill using Azure OpenAI Realtime API.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Authentication
medium_findings.description: Skill accesses AZURE_OPENAI_AUDIO_API_KEY from environment. This is legitimate behavior for Azure OpenAI integration - API keys are required for authentication. No user input is concatenated with these values.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable access is a standard pattern for API authentication. The skill only reads predefined Azure environment variables, not user-controlled input.

medium_findings.title: Network Access to Azure OpenAI Endpoint
medium_findings.description: Skill connects to Azure OpenAI WebSocket endpoint for realtime audio generation. This is expected behavior for a TTS service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access to Azure's official cognitive services endpoint is documented and expected. The URL shown is a placeholder format example, not a real endpoint.

medium_findings.title: Base64 Encoding for Audio Data
medium_findings.description: Skill uses base64 encoding/decoding for audio PCM chunks. This is standard practice for transferring binary audio data over text-based protocols.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Base64 is the correct way to encode binary audio data for WebSocket transmission. No obfuscation or evasion intent detected.

medium_findings.title: Code Fences Detected as Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer misidentified markdown backtick code fences as Ruby/shell backtick execution. The backticks are markdown delimiters for code blocks, not actual shell commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Clear false positive - the SKILL.md file contains markdown with fenced code blocks (Python, JavaScript, bash). The backticks are part of markdown syntax, not executable commands.

low_findings.title: Misidentified Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged YAML frontmatter and plain text references as weak cryptographic algorithms. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML frontmatter 'name:', line 121 is a file path reference, line 126 is plain text. No cryptographic algorithms present.

content.user_title: Generate AI Podcast Audio from Text
content.value_statement: This skill enables developers to create podcast-style audio narratives using Azure OpenAI's GPT Realtime Mini model. It handles WebSocket connections, audio streaming, and format conversion to produce natural-sounding speech.
actual_capabilities: Connect to Azure OpenAI Realtime API via WebSocket for streaming audio
Convert text prompts into spoken audio using GPT Realtime Mini model
Stream audio chunks in real-time with minimal latency
Support multiple voice options including alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, and shimmer
Convert PCM audio data to WAV format for browser playback
limitations: Requires Azure OpenAI subscription and API credentials
Audio quality depends on Azure service availability
Requires stable WebSocket connection for streaming

use_cases.title: Content Creator Audio Blog
use_cases.description: Transform blog posts or articles into podcast-style audio for accessibility and wider reach.

use_cases.title: E-Learning Audio Narration
use_cases.description: Generate spoken audio narrations for educational content and online courses.

use_cases.title: Accessibility Audio Tool
use_cases.description: Convert text-based UI content to speech for screen reader compatibility.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text to Speech
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this text to podcast-style audio using the Azure OpenAI Realtime API: {text_input}

prompt_templates.title: Custom Voice Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate audio narration with the {voice_name} voice for this content: {text_content}

prompt_templates.title: Audio with Transcript
prompt_templates.prompt: Create podcast audio and transcript for: {article_content}. Include both audio output and text transcript.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Content Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Process multiple text segments into audio files. Segment 1: {text1}, Segment 2: {text2}, Segment 3: {text3}. Generate individual audio files for each.
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting multiple text pieces to audio

output_examples.input: Generate a podcast introduction for a tech news show
output_examples.output: Base64-encoded WAV audio file ready for playback in browser, with transcript showing the generated narration text

output_examples.input: Convert this article about AI advancements into audio
output_examples.output: Streaming audio chunks delivered via WebSocket, combined into a complete WAV file with synchronized transcript

best_practices: Configure Azure environment variables before using the skill for authentication
Use appropriate voice selection based on content type (onyx for formal, fable for expressive)
Handle WebSocket connection errors gracefully with retry logic
anti_patterns: Do not expose API keys in client-side code - keep them server-side only
Avoid sending sensitive or personal information in text prompts without encryption
Do not assume infinite API quota - implement rate limiting and caching

faq.question: What Azure services does this skill require?
faq.answer: This skill requires Azure OpenAI with GPT Realtime Mini deployment and valid API credentials.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: No, this skill requires an active internet connection to communicate with Azure OpenAI services.

faq.question: What audio format does the skill output?
faq.answer: The skill outputs WAV format audio (24kHz, 16-bit, mono) encoded as base64 for easy transfer.

faq.question: How many voices are available?
faq.answer: Six voices are supported: alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, and shimmer.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for production use?
faq.answer: Yes, with proper error handling and API key management, this can be used in production applications.

faq.question: Does this skill store any audio data?
faq.answer: No, the skill processes audio in memory and returns it to the caller without persistent storage.
